title: MCP Resources
concept: Read-only data a server exposes (files, records, live data) that the host can pull into context without a full tool call.

exercises.prompt: What are MCP Tools, which we covered in the previous lesson?

options.text: They are callable functions with typed inputs and outputs that the model can invoke.
They are static prompts stored on a local text file.

exercises.explain: In MCP, Tools are active, callable functions with inputs and outputs that let the model perform actions, similar to classic function calling.

cards.title: Read-Only Power
cards.body: In the last lesson, we saw how tools let AI take action. But what if the AI just needs to read a file or check status updates without changing anything? That's where resources come in!

cards.title: What is an MCP Resource?
cards.body: MCP Resources are read-only data sources that a server makes available to the host application. They allow the LLM to pull in files, database records, or live application data directly into its conversation context.

cards.title: The Bulletin Board Analogy
cards.body: Think of MCP Tools like a vending machine where you press buttons to make things happen. MCP Resources are like a bulletin board: the information is just posted there for you to read whenever you need it, without needing to press any buttons.

cards.title: A Real-World Example
cards.body: An MCP server can expose a resource URI like 'postgres://database/users/123'. The host application can fetch this resource to show the LLM a user's profile details before answering a question.

cards.title: Not Just Static Text
cards.body: You might think resources are just static text files. In reality, they can represent dynamic, live-updating data like a system's current CPU usage or the latest comments on a blog post.

cards.title: Tools vs. Resources
cards.body: Understanding the difference between active tools and passive, readable resources.

left.text: Active function call: can write data, run commands, or trigger actions.

right.text: Read-only data: fetches files, logs, or live system state safely.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of MCP Resources?

options.text: They allow the AI to execute arbitrary code on the host machine.
They expose read-only data like files and live records to the host.
They delete database records to save server disk space.
They encrypt the model's weights during inference.

exercises.explain: MCP Resources are designed specifically to expose read-only data such as files, database records, and live status feeds directly to the host application.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An AI can use an MCP Resource to write new data and execute commands on a server.
exercises.explain: MCP Resources are strictly read-only; they do not allow the model to execute functions or modify server states.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the nature of MCP Resources.
exercises.sentence: MCP Resources act as read-only data paths that let the AI pull in information safely.
exercises.explain: MCP Resources provide read-only access to data, ensuring the model can safely inspect files or records without accidentally modifying them.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements about MCP is correct?

options.text: MCP Resources are code blocks that the AI runs to delete user accounts.
MCP Resources are active plugins that intercept and block all tool calls.
MCP Resources are read-only data templates, whereas tools are callable functions.

exercises.explain: Option C shows an AI correctly distinguishing that MCP Resources are read-only data, while MCP Tools are active functions that can modify state or perform tasks.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building an AI assistant. Which of the following tasks is the best fit for an MCP Resource rather than an MCP Tool?

options.text: It executes a Python script to calculate new values on the fly.
It modifies a file on the server and saves the changes.
It pulls in a read-only server log file to use as context.
It triggers an API call that deletes an old user database record.

exercises.explain: MCP Resources are designed to expose read-only data, such as a file, a database record, or live metrics. Because they are read-only, they do not require the model to decide on and execute a function call that alters system state.
